page: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by a 1843 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing scoring in the top 25% at 85051.53. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth is also strong at 176.14 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reach a strong tier of 62.24 million operations per second. Software compilation using Clang via Geekbench also registers in the top 25% at 39975.0. Conversely, single-core capabilities are more limited, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the D48plds_v5 is its low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive workloads. However, the dedicated physical cores and high multi-core efficiency provide a cost-effective profile for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, web serving, database operations, and lightweight multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPLDSv5 family, utilizing 48 dedicated physical cores of an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes a 1843 GB local disk. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core tests, large-block memory reads, and software compilation. However, single-core performance and text processing are relatively weak. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-focused, multi-threaded applications that do not demand high memory capacity. It is suited for software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM64 server with 48 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 96 GB RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM64 server featuring 48 Ampere cores, 96 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-focused server featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and local storage for efficient multi-threaded processing.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 3.0 GHz.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth.
Low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core presents a memory-capacity tradeoff.
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
